message:
fix(coordinator): add lifecycle mutex to serialize Start/Stop, eliminate shared ctx field race

Add lifecycleMu sync.Mutex to SessionCoordinator so Start() and Stop()
cannot interleave -- completing the systematic lifecycle mutex sweep
across all NTM subsystems (resilience monitor, autoscanner, timeline,
persister, supervisor, and now coordinator).

Key changes:
- Both Start() and Stop() acquire lifecycleMu first, serializing the
  two operations
- Removed stored ctx context.Context field from the struct; ctx and
  stopCh are now passed directly as parameters to monitorLoop() and
  digestLoop(), eliminating a shared mutable field that could be
  read/written concurrently
- c.started = false moved earlier in Stop() -- now set before
  c.mu.Unlock() (inside the first critical section) rather than after
  wg.Wait(), preventing a window where started is still true while the
  coordinator is mid-teardown

New test TestStop_WaitsForConcurrentStartInitialization verifies that
Stop() blocks until a concurrent Start() finishes its initialization
(the initial updateAgentStates call), using a mock
getPanesWithActivity to block Start mid-init and asserting Stop does
not return prematurely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
